instruction:
An ant starts at $(1,1)$ in the Cartesian plane. Every second, the ant moves from its position $(x,y)$ to either $(x+1,y)$ or $(x,y+1)$ . However, it cannot ever move to a point whose $x-$ and $y-$ coordinates are both divisible by $3$ . Let $N$ be the number of paths that the ant can take from $(1,1)$ to $(11,11)$ . Find the remainder when $N$ is divided by $1000$ .

Options:
A. 491
B. 499
C. 494
D. 493
E. 498
F. 497
G. 496
H. 490
I. 492
J. 495
